chunks.text:
# Manufacturing Business Glossary

This glossary defines the core business concepts and terminology used in the manufacturing supply chain domain. It covers product structures, materials management, production workflows, and quality assurance processes.

## Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex product... [TRUNCATED]

## Warehouse

A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique warehouse identifier
- Warehouse name and location
- Address, city, and ... [TRUNCATED]

## QualityControl

Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods. QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique QC identifier
- Batch reference (pr... [TRUNCATED]

# Manufacturing Data Dictionary

This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system. It includes all tables, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships. Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures.

## product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key.

| Column | Da... [TRUNCATED]

## bom

Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure. Supports recursive relationships for multi-level assemblies.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| bom_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique BOM identifier |
| parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) |
| component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → produc... [TRUNCATED]

## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier |
| warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name |
| address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |
| city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |
| state | VARCHAR(50) | | State or province |
| capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CH... [TRUNCATED]

## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |
| product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |
| parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition... [TRUNCATED]

## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique specification identifier |
| specification_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Specification name |
| version | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL | Specification version |
| effective_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Effective date |
| spec_type | spec_type_t | ... [TRUNCATED]

## shipment

Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| shipment_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique shipment identifier |
| shipment_type | shipment_type_t | NOT NULL | INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL |
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse reference |
| supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | S... [TRUNCATED]

triplets.subject: Product
triplets.object: any manufactured item in the system

triplets.subject: Product
triplets.object: lead time for procurement or production

triplets.subject: Component
triplets.object: atomic raw materials or purchased parts

triplets.subject: Warehouse
triplets.object: node in the supply chain network

triplets.subject: Specification
triplets.object: technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria

entities_post_resolution.name: Ea (Each)
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unit of measure representing 'each' used to count individual items in inventory or transactional data.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure | | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.) |

entities_post_resolution.name: Production Batch
entities_post_resolution.definition: A production batch (also known as a lot) is a specific quantity of product produced in a single manufacturing run, tracked for traceability, inventory control, and quality assurance.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Batch represents a specific production lot or run of a product, tracking all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle. | Tracks production lots for traceability and inventory management. | Indexes:
- `idx_batch_product` on product_id | Indexes:
- `idx_batch_date` on production_date | Indexes:
- `idx_batch_warehouse` on warehouse_id | Indexes:
- `idx_batch_qc_status` on qc_status | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches undergo Quality Control inspections | One-to-many with batch

entities_post_resolution.name: Component
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Component is a basic building block used in manufacturing, representing atomic raw materials or purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items. | Components are the building blocks used in manufacturing and are procured from external suppliers. | Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements. | may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements. | Components are supplied by multiple Suppliers (many-to-many) | Components have Inventory records tracking stock levels | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements | Suppliers provide multiple Components (many-to-many via component_supplier)

entities_post_resolution.name: Component Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A component product is an item included as a part of a parent product in a bill of materials, which can be a component or sub-assembly required to assemble the parent product.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it) |

entities_post_resolution.name: Inspection Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The date on which an inspection was performed, as recorded in quality control (QC) records.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | qc_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Inspection date | | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.name: Last Restock Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The date of the most recent restock event for an inventory item or component.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | last_restock_date | DATE | | Last restock timestamp | | Inventory represents the actual stock levels of components and products stored in specific warehouses. Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement. Inventory records support either components or products (but not both in the same record) and include timestamps for the last restock date.

entities_post_resolution.name: Parent Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: The parent product is the assembly or sub-assembly that contains one or more component products within a bill of materials or product structure, i.e., the item being built.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) | | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure.

entities_post_resolution.name: Planned End Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: A DATE attribute representing the planned (target) end date for a project, task, or activity.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | planned_end_date | DATE | | Planned end date |

entities_post_resolution.name: Planned Start Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The planned_start_date is the scheduled date on which a project, task, or activity is intended to commence.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | planned_start_date | DATE | | Planned start date |

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: Product is the manufactured item in the supply chain system, including finished goods, sub-assemblies, and components, uniquely identifiable and capable of hierarchical relationships (e.g., parent-child via BOM / self-referencing structures).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | Hierarchical relationship via parent_product_id | Products can be active or inactive based on current manufacturing requirements. | A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy) | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | Products are used in Work Orders for production | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Products are stored in Inventory records | Work Orders produce Products | BOM connects Products to other Products (parent-child) | # Manufacturing Data Dictionary

This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system. It includes all tables, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships. Special attention

entities_post_resolution.name: Production Batch
entities_post_resolution.definition: A production batch is a discrete group of items produced in a single manufacturing run, identified by a batch reference, that undergoes QC inspections, tests against specifications, and status decisions.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| batch_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Production batch reference | | **Relationships:**
- QC inspections are performed on Batches
- QC tests against Specifications
- QC results determine Batch status
- QC failures trigger rework or scrap decisions

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity Completed
entities_post_resolution.definition: A decimal field representing the quantity completed for an order, non-negative and not exceeding the ordered quantity.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_completed | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0, <= ordered | Quantity completed |

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Shipment represents the record of a physical transfer of materials between locations (inbound from suppliers to warehouses and outbound to customers), including shipment type, source and destination, dates, status, and related tracking information.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Shipment represents the physical transfer of materials between warehouses, from suppliers to warehouses (inbound), or from warehouses to customers (outbound). | Shipments track shipment type, source and destination locations, dates for ship, estimated arrival, and actual delivery, along with current status. | Shipments update Inventory levels | Shipments fulfill Work Order outputs | Products appear in Shipments (outbound)

entities_post_resolution.name: Specification
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Specification is a formal document that defines technical requirements, standards, acceptance criteria, and quality requirements for materials, products, or components, used to guide design, production, and verification.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Specification defines technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products. |  | specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Specification being tested | | Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products. | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements | Specifications define requirements for Components | Specifications are used in Quality Control inspections | - Optional relationship with specification

entities_post_resolution.name: Warehouse
entities_post_resolution.definition: A warehouse is a physical storage facility used to store materials, components, and finished goods, with defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and designated management.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. | Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. | Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution. | Work Orders are associated with Warehouses | Stores physical storage location information for inventory management. | One-to-many with inventory | One-to-many with shipment | One-to-many with work_order | One-to-many with batch

entities_post_resolution.name: Atomic Raw Materials
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Component representing atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items.
synonyms: atomic raw materials and purchased parts
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items. | ## component

Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers.

entities_post_resolution.name: Base Manufacturing Cost
entities_post_resolution.definition: The base manufacturing cost is the standard per-unit production cost used as the starting point for pricing and cost planning.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | | base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost |

entities_post_resolution.name: Component Supplier Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique identifier that serves as the primary key for a component and is used to reference the component in related records.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | component_supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique relationship identifier | | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique component identifier | | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference | | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference (exclusive with product_id) | | Indexes: - `idx_inventory_component` on component_id (WHERE NOT NULL) | | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it) |

entities_post_resolution.name: Product Type
entities_post_resolution.definition: A product classification attribute indicating the kind of product: FINISHED_GOOD, ASSEMBLY, or COMPONENT.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | product_type | product_type_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'FINISHED_GOOD' | Product classification: FINISHED_GOOD, ASSEMBLY, COMPONENT |

entities_post_resolution.name: Production Lot
entities_post_resolution.definition: A production lot is a batch of product units produced in a single manufacturing run, tracked for traceability and inventory management.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Batch represents a specific production lot or run of a product, tracking all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle. | Tracks production lots for traceability and inventory management.

entities_post_resolution.name: Qc Status T
entities_post_resolution.definition: An enumerated quality-control status type used to indicate the outcome of QC checks, with values PENDING, PASSED, FAILED, and QUARANTINED.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | qc_status | qc_status_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'PENDING' | PENDING, PASSED, FAILED, QUARANTINED |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity On Hand
entities_post_resolution.definition: Quantity on hand (quantity_on_hand) is the current stock level of inventory stored in warehouses that is available for fulfillment.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity | | Inventory represents the actual stock levels of components and products stored in specific warehouses. Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement.

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Shipment represents the transfer of goods or inventory between supply chain locations (warehouses, suppliers, and customers) as a logical unit in logistics.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Shipment represents the physical transfer of materials between warehouses, from suppliers to warehouses (inbound), or from warehouses to customers (outbound). | Shipments track shipment type, source and destination locations, dates for ship, estimated arrival, and actual delivery, along with current status. | Shipments update Inventory levels | Shipments fulfill Work Order outputs | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | One-to-many with shipment | Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers. | Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

entities_post_resolution.name: Standard Procurement Cost
entities_post_resolution.definition: A predefined cost benchmark used for budgeting and procurement of components or materials, against which actual procurement costs are measured.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements. | | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost |

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Of Measure
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unit of measure (UOM) used to quantify item quantities in manufacturing BOMs and inventory records, such as EA, KG, M, L.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.) | | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure | | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | | Test unit of measure | | Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements. | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure.

entities_post_resolution.name: Warehouse(Warehouse Id)
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique identifier for a warehouse, used as the key in the warehouse table and as a foreign key reference from other tables.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse location | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Production location | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse reference | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier | | Indexes: - `idx_inventory_warehouse` on warehouse_id | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Storage location |

entities_post_resolution.name: Work Order Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: The unique identifier (primary key) of a work order, used to reference a specific work order in the system and as the target of foreign-key relationships.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier | | | parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition |

entities_post_resolution.name: Null Default
entities_post_resolution.definition: A numeric field named quantity that is NOT NULL, defaults to 1.000, and must be greater than 0, representing the required quantity in inventory or procurement.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity | DECIMAL(10,3) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1.000, CHECK > 0 | Required quantity | | | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity | | | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Procurement or production lead time | | | base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost | | | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost | | | specification_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Specification name | | | version | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL | Specification version | | | effective_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Effective date | | | spec_type | spec_type_t | NOT NULL | DIMENSIONAL, MATERIAL, PERFORMANCE | | | production_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Production date | | | route_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Route name | | | is_active | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'Y' | Active status flag |

entities_post_resolution.name: Decimal(10,2)
entities_post_resolution.definition: A SQL character varying column type for storing text with a maximum length of 100 characters.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_produced | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK > 0 | Quantity produced | | | min_value | DECIMAL(10,3) | | Minimum acceptable value | | | max_value | DECIMAL(10,3) | | Maximum acceptable value | | | cycle_time_minutes | DECIMAL(8,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Per-unit processing time | | | setup_time_minutes | DECIMAL(8,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Fixed setup time | | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | | Test unit of measure | | | critical_parameter | VARCHAR(100) | | Parameter being tested | | | operation_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Operation description | | | specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Technical specification reference | | | bom_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique BOM identifier | | | work_center | VARCHAR(50) | | Work center location |

entities_post_resolution.name: Defect Count
entities_post_resolution.definition: A non-negative integer field representing the total number of defects found in a product or process during inspection.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | defect_count | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Number of defects found | | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.name: Cycle Time Minutes
entities_post_resolution.definition: The time, in minutes, required to process a single unit of product (per-unit processing time).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | cycle_time_minutes | DECIMAL(8,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Per-unit processing time | | | setup_time_minutes | DECIMAL(8,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Fixed setup time |

entities_post_resolution.name: Lead Time For
entities_post_resolution.definition: The duration required to procure or produce a product.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Procurement or production lead time | | | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0 | Procurement lead time |

entities_post_resolution.name: Material Transfers Between
entities_post_resolution.definition: An operational concept representing the physical movement of materials across the supply chain between suppliers, warehouses, and customers, typically captured as inbound and outbound shipments.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers. | A Shipment represents the physical transfer of materials between warehouses, from suppliers to warehouses (inbound), or from warehouses to customers (outbound).

entities_post_resolution.definition: max_value is the upper bound defining the maximum permissible value for a numeric field in validation or constraint rules.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | max_value | DECIMAL(10,3) | | Maximum acceptable value | | | min_value | DECIMAL(10,3) | | Minimum acceptable value |

entities_post_resolution.definition: A numeric quantity in inventory records that specifies the stock level at which a replenishment order should be triggered.

Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement.

entities_post_resolution.name: Qc
entities_post_resolution.definition: Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods.

entities_post_resolution.name: Qc Activities
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Relationships:**
- QC inspections are performed on Batches
- QC tests against Specifications
- QC results determine Batch status
- QC failures trigger rework or scrap decisions
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Relationships:**
- QC inspections are performed on Batches
- QC tests against Specifications
- QC results determine Batch status
- QC failures trigger rework or scrap decisions

entities_post_resolution.name: Qc Records
entities_post_resolution.definition: QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.name: All Manufactured Items
entities_post_resolution.definition:
# Manufacturing Data Dictionary

This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system. It includes all tables, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships. Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures.

## product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
# Manufacturing Data Dictionary

This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system. It includes all tables, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships. Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures.

## product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key.

entities_post_resolution.name: Manufactured Item In
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products, including identifiers, names, types, hierarchical relationships to parent products, cost basis, lead time for fulfillment, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Component
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about individual components, including identification, naming, category, measurement unit, cost, and associated specification.

tables_enriched.name: Bill of Materials
tables_enriched.description: Stores the structure of the Bill of Materials, listing components required to build a parent product along with quantities and whether each component is optional.

tables_enriched.name: Supplier
tables_enriched.description: Stores data about suppliers, including contact details and a preferred supplier indicator.

tables_enriched.name: Warehouse
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about each warehouse, including its identity, location, capacity, and the manager responsible for the facility.

tables_enriched.name: Inventory
tables_enriched.description: Stores current inventory levels and restocking information for products across warehouses, including on-hand quantities, reserved quantities, and reorder thresholds.

tables_enriched.name: Work Order
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of work orders for products, including quantities, status, priority, planned timeline, and associated warehouse.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores shipment records detailing what is being shipped, who is supplying it, where it is coming from, and timing milestones including ship date, estimated and actual arrival, along with the shipment ... [TRUNCATED]

tables_enriched.name: Quality Control
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of quality control checks performed on batches, including the type of check, inspector, date, result, defect counts, and any notes.

tables_enriched.name: Specification
tables_enriched.description: Stores specifications and their valid ranges or thresholds, including type, effective date, and measurement units used for validation or configuration in business processes.

tables_enriched.name: Batch
tables_enriched.description: Stores batch-level production data, including which product was produced, the quantity, production and expiry dates, the warehouse location, and quality control status.

tables_enriched.name: Route
tables_enriched.description: Stores the sequence of operations and timing for a product route, including the route identifiers, associated product, operation details, and the time required for setup and cycle execution.

tables_enriched.name: Component Supplier
tables_enriched.description: Stores the relationship between components and suppliers, including whether a supplier is preferred and the lead time and price for providing a specific component.
